title: DesignPackageManifest
description:
A portable design-system package manifest (`manifest.json`).

The manifest is the discovery + trust root: the registry reads it, resolves every `files` path under the package directory, and verifies each declared `content_hashes` entry. `license` is mandatory — a package with no license cannot be trusted or redistributed, so the registry rejects an empty one.

content_hashes.description: Declared content hashes the registry verifies against the on-disk files.

description.description: One-line description of the package's intent.

files.description: Where the design / tokens / components documents live, package-relative.

id.description: Stable package identifier (the registry resolves packages by this id).

license.description: SPDX-style license identifier. Mandatory and non-empty.

package_schema_version.description: Schema/format revision of the package layout itself (monotonic).

platforms.description: Target platforms this package can compile adapters for.

version.description: Semantic version string of the package contents.

DesignContentHash.description: A single declared content hash binding a package-relative file to a stable digest. The registry recomputes the digest from the on-disk bytes and rejects the package on any mismatch.

hash.description: Stable digest of the file bytes (`fnv1a64:<hex>`).

DesignPackageFiles.description: Where a package's tokens/components/design files are found, relative to the package directory. Absolute paths and `..` are contract violations the registry rejects; the shapes only ever hold package-relative strings.

components.description: Package-relative path to the optional component catalog.

design.description: Package-relative path to the prose `DESIGN` document (e.g. `DESIGN.md`).

tokens.description: Package-relative path to the token document (e.g. `tokens.json`).

usage.description: Package-relative path to an optional usage / examples document.

DesignPackageSecurity.description: Declared security posture of a package's assets. A package that claims it carries no executable code or remote URLs is held to that claim by importers.

DesignPackageSource.description: Provenance of an imported package, redacted of any userinfo/secret material.
